questions.text: The lithospheric plates move because of the movement of:
questions.explanation: The molten magma inside the earth moves in a circular manner, causing plates to shift.

questions.text: Sudden movements in the earth's crust cause:
questions.explanation: Endogenic forces sometimes produce sudden movements like earthquakes which cause mass destruction.

questions.text: The place on the surface above the focus of an earthquake is called the:
questions.explanation: Vibrations travel outwards from the epicentre as waves; greatest damage is usually closest to it.

questions.text: As the river enters the plain it twists and turns forming large bends known as:
questions.explanation: Due to continuous erosion and deposition along the sides of the bend, meanders are formed.

questions.text: Which landform is created by the depositional work of sea waves?
questions.explanation: Sea waves deposit sediments along the shores to form beaches.

questions.text: Glacial moraines are formed by the ________ of glaciers.
questions.explanation: The material carried by the glacier such as rocks, sand and silt gets deposited to form moraines.

questions.text: Mushroom rocks are commonly found in:
questions.explanation: In deserts, wind erodes the lower section of the rock more than the upper part, creating a mushroom shape.

questions.text: When the meander loop is cut off from the main river, it forms a:
questions.explanation: Over time, the meander loop cuts off from the river and forms a cutoff lake, also called an ox-bow lake.

questions.text: Steep rocky coasts rising almost vertically above sea water are called:
questions.explanation: Continuous erosion of coastal rocks results in the formation of sea cliffs.

questions.text: The collection of sediments from all the mouths of a river forms a:
questions.explanation: Before entering the sea, a river breaks into distributaries and deposits its load to form a delta.
